expectations.text: Clearly declares which prompt is better (should be B)
expectations.evidence: 'Verdict: Prompt B is significantly better' clearly stated

expectations.text: Provides numerical scores for both prompts across dimensions
expectations.evidence: Uses a 1-5 scale for 5 criteria (Role clarity, Scope specificity, Output structure, Actionability, Reproducibility) but not the 6-dimension framework with numeric scores

expectations.text: Shows per-dimension differences between the two prompts
expectations.evidence: Compares across 5 qualitative criteria (1/5 vs 5/5) but doesn't show 6-dimension deltas with numeric scores

expectations.text: Explains specifically WHY the winner is better with concrete reasoning
expectations.evidence: 5 detailed sections: Role Assignment, Scope and Specificity, Output Format Constraint, Actionability, Reproducibility, plus cognitive load analysis

expectations.text: Identifies the prompting techniques used in Prompt B (Role Assignment, Structured Output, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Names 3 techniques: Persona framing, Constrained scope with examples, Structured output specification

expectations.text: Suggests how the winning prompt could be further improved
expectations.evidence: No specific improvement suggestions for Prompt B
